questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q:
Let $x, y$ be real numbers such that $x y=1$. Let $T$ and $t$ be the largest and the smallest values of the expression $\frac{(x+y)^2-(x-y)-2}{(x+y)^2+(x-y)-2}$

If $T+t$ can be expressed in the form $\frac{m}{n}$ where $m, n$ are nonzero integers with $\operatorname{GCD}(m, n)=1$, find the value of $m+n$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x y=1 \Rightarrow(x+y)^2=(x-y)^2+4 \\ & \frac{(x+y)^2-(x-y)-2}{(x+y)^2+(x-y)-2}=\frac{(x-y)^2-(x-y)+2}{(x-y)^2+(x-y)+2} \\ & =\frac{\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^2-\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)+2}{\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^2+\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)+2} \\ & x-\frac{1}{x} \in R \\ & \text { Let } f(t)=\frac{t^2-t+2}{t^2+t+2}, t \in R\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & f(t)_{\max }+f(t)_{\min }=\frac{4+\sqrt{2}}{4-\sqrt{2}}+\frac{4-\sqrt{2}}{4+\sqrt{2}}=\frac{18}{7}=\frac{m}{n} \\ & m+n=25\end{aligned}$
